steps.title: Identify what an x-intercept is
steps.content: The x-intercept is the point where the graph crosses the x-axis. At this point, $y = 0$.

steps.title: Read the x-coordinate from the graph
steps.content: Locate where the curve intersects the x-axis. Tracing the intersection point straight down to the x-axis, I find that the graph crosses at $x = 5$. The x-intercept is the point $(5, 0)$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
